headline: Air Pollution Costs Malaysia RM12.6B in Healthcare Annually
context: A 2025 Health Ministry-commissioned study estimated the annual healthcare cost of air pollution-related illness in Malaysia at RM12.6 billion, including premature deaths.

cards.big: Air pollution kills an estimated 15,000 Malaysians prematurely each year.
cards.sub: Healthcare costs: RM12.6B annually. Total DOE enforcement budget: RM42M — 0.3% of the damage.

cards.big: PM2.5 levels in KL exceed WHO guidelines on 245 days per year.
cards.sub: Malaysia uses its own less stringent air quality standard. Under WHO guidelines, KL fails 67% of the time.

cards.big: Vehicle emissions account for 75% of urban PM2.5.
cards.sub: Euro 4 emission standards were adopted in 2015. The EU moved to Euro 6d in 2020.

cards.big: B40 households are 2.3x more likely to live near high-pollution roads.
cards.sub: Environmental health burden falls disproportionately on those least able to afford healthcare.

cards.big: RM12.6B in health damage versus RM42M in enforcement. We spend 300 times more treating pollution than preventing it.
Air quality is a health policy issue, not just an environmental one. Aligning Malaysia's PM2.5 standards with WHO would force action that saves both lives and healthcare costs.
